name: Skitter Smack
effects: The user Shifts up to its Overland Capability, ignoring Attacks of Opportunity, before attacking with Skitter Smack. On a hit, the target’s Special Attack is lowered 1 CS. Contest

name: False Surrender
effects: False Surrender cannot miss. Contest

name: Fiery Wrath
effects: Fiery Wrath Flinches the target on a 17+. Once per Scene, Fiery Wrath may instead be used as a Fire- Type Move. Contest

name: Jaw Lock
effects: You may perform a Grapple Maneuver against the target as a Free Action. Contest

name: Lash Out
effects: If the user has lost CS from a source other than itself since the end of its last turn, Lash Out is instead DB 15 (4d10 + 20 / 45). Any given foe may be the target of this increased damage only once per Scene. Contest

name: Obstruct
effects: If the user is hit by a Move, the user may use Obstruct. The user is instead not hit by the Move. You do not take any damage nor are you affected by any of the Move’s effects. In addition, if the triggering attack was Melee ranged, the attacker’s Defense is lowered by 2 Combat Stages. Contest

name: Wicked Blow
effects: If Wicked Blow hits, it is a Critical Hit. The target is Pushed 6 meters minus their Weight Class. On a roll of 15+, the target is also Tripped. Contest

name: Breaking Swipe
effects: The target’s Attack is lowered 1 CS. Contest

name: Clangorous Soul
effects: The user loses 1/3rd of their Max Hit Points and has each of its stats raised by +1 Combat Stage. Contest

name: Dragon Darts
range: 6, 1 Target, Double Strike; or 6, 2 Targets Contest
effects: Double Time

name: Dragon Energy
effects: For each 10% of HP the user is missing, Dragon Energy’s Damage Base is reduced by 1. Contest

name: Dynamax Cannon
effects: The DB of Dynamax Cannon increases by +2 for each positive CS the target has, to a maximum of DB 20. Contest

name: Eternabeam
effects: Seen Nothing Yet

name: Scale Shot
effects: The user’s Speed is raised 1 CS, and the user’s Defense is lowered 1 CS. Contest

name: Aura Wheel
effects: The user’s Speed is increased 1 CS. Contest

name: Bolt Beak
effects: Against targets with a lower initiative that have not yet acted this round, Bolt Beak deals +10 damage. Contest

name: Magnetic Flux
effects: Choose +1 or -1. All targets that are Electric- Type or have the Magnetic Capability receive that many Defense and Special Defense Combat Stages. Contest

name: Overdrive
effects: Attention Grabber

name: Rising Voltage
effects: After Rising Voltage is resolved, you may create Electric Terrain in a Blast 7, centered on the user, that lasts for 5 turns. Any creatures in those spaces are affected as if by the Field Move Electric Terrain, rather than any other Field Move. This Effect may trigger only once per Scene. Contest

name: Thunder Cage
effects: The targets are trapped in a Vortex. The DC to escape the Vortex is increased by 3. Contest

name: Decorate
effects: The target gains +2 CS in both Attack and Special Attack. Contest

name: Misty Explosion
effects: The user’s Hit Points are set to -50% of their full Hit Point value. This Hit Point loss cannot be prevented or reduced in any way. After Misty Explosion is resolved, create Misty Terrain in a Blast 7, centered on the user, that lasts for 5 turns. Any creatures in those spaces are affected as if by the Field Move Misty Terrain, rather than any other Field Move. Contest

name: Spirit Break
effects: The target’s Special Attack stat is lowered 1 CS. Contest

name: Strange Steam
effects: The target is confused on 17+. Contest

name: Body Press
effects: The user’s Defense Stat is added to the damage roll instead of the Attack Stat. Contest

name: Coaching
effects: The user and any allies in the Burst have their Attack and Defense raised by 1 CS. Contest

name: Meteor Assault
effects: Exhausting Act

name: No Retreat
effects: The user gains +1 CS in each stat. In addition, the user may not be recalled or switched out until the end of the Scene, and counts as Slowed for the purposes of any movement that would end further away from the nearest enemy than the user started. Contest

name: Octolock
effects: The user initiates a Grapple Maneuver with the target, which automatically hits. If successful, until the user no longer has Dominance in the grapple, the target is Trapped and loses 1 CS in Defense and Special Defense at the end of each of their turns. Contest

name: Thunderous Kick
effects: All legal targets hit lose 1 Defense Combat Stage. Once per Scene, Thunderous Kick may instead be used as an Electric-Type Move. Contest

name: Burning Jealousy
effects: After Burning Jealousy has resolved, the user may choose to Burn foes within 5m that have had any CS raised since the beginning of that foe’s last turn. This Effect may trigger only once per Scene. Contest

name: Pyro Ball
effects: The target is Burned on 19+. Contest

name: Dual Wingbeat
effects: Reliable Ghost

name: Astral Barrage
effects: Legal targets hit by Astral Barrage are Slowed for one full round. Contest

name: Poltergeist
effects: If a target is holding a Held Item or Main or Off- Hand item, they must either drop it immediately or lose a Tick of Hit Points. This may only cause a target to lose at most one Tick of Hit Points, no matter how many items they were holding. On each of the target’s subsequent turns, if it is still holding the item(s) in question it loses a Tick of Hit Points. Contest

name: Apple Acid
effects: The target’s Special Defense is lowered by 1 CS. Contest

name: Branch Poke
effects: Steady Performance

name: Drum Beating
effects: The target’s Speed is lowered by 1 CS. Contest

name: Grassy Glide
effects: If the user is on Grassy Terrain, Grassy Glide may be used with Priority. After Grassy Glide has been resolved, the user may choose to create Grassy Terrain in the spaces Passed over (including those it started and ended in), that lasts for 5 turns. Any creatures in those spaces are affected as if by the Field Move Grassy Terrain, rather than any other Field Move. Contest

name: Grav Apple
effects: The target’s Defense is lowered by 1 CS. Contest

name: Jungle Healing
effects: The user and any allies in the burst regain Hit Points equal to 1/4 of their Maximum Hit Point Value, and are cured of any Persistent or Volatile Status Conditions. Contest

name: Snap Trap
effects: The target is trapped in a Vortex. The DC to escape the Vortex is increased by 3. Contest

name: Scorching Sands
effects: Scorching Sands Burns the target on 15+. Contest

name: Glacial Lance
effects: Legal targets hit by Glacial Lance are Stuck and Trapped until the end of their next turn. Contest

name: Triple Axel
effects: After attacking with Triple Axel, hit or miss, the user may Disengage 2m and attack a different target with Triple Axel. This effect may be repeated a second time, targeting a creature that has not been targeted by either prior attack. Before making each attack roll, the user can elect to give up triggering all remaining additional shifts and attacks. Triple Axel gains +1 Accuracy and +1 DB for each attack that is given up. Triple Axel always benefits from the Technician Ability. Contest

name: Court Change
effects: All Blessings and Hazards swap which side that they belong to. Contest

name: Howl
effects: The user and all allies in the Burst have their Attack CS raised by 1. Contest

name: Multi-Attack
effects: Multi-Attack is the same Type as the Memory Disc, Plate, or Drive Item held. Contest

name: Rapid Spin
effects: Rapid Spin destroys all Hazards within 5 meters, removes Leech Seeds, and removes the user’s Trapped or Stuck status. If Rapid Spin hits, the user’s speed raises 1 CS. Contest

name: Stuff Cheeks
effects: The user immediately consumes one of its Food Buffs, regardless of any conditions that need to be met, gaining its benefit if applicable. If a Buff is consumed, the user’s Defense CS is raised by 2. Contest

name: Teatime
effects: All participants in the battle may choose to immediately consume one of their Food Buffs, regardless of if the conditions to do so are met. Contest

name: Terrain Pulse
effects: If the user is under the effects of a Terrain, Terrain Pulse has a Damage Base of 10 (3d8+10 / 24). In addition, Terrain Pulse becomes the Type that corresponds with the Move that creates that Terrain. (For example, if in Misty Terrain, it would become Fairy Type.) If the user is under the effects of multiple Terrains, choose one to affect this Move. Contest

name: Corrosive Gas
effects: Targets hit have their current Held Item(s) unable to be used for the remainder of the Scene. In addition, Steel-Type targets hit lose their immunity to Poison until they Faint or Take A Breather; Poison instead counts as neutral against Steel. Contest

name: Shell Side Arm
effects: This attack uses the highest of the user’s Attack or Special Attack Stat, though this does not change the Class of the Move. In addition, if the target’s Defense is lower than its Special Defense, this attack targets Defense, becoming a Physical Move. The target is poisoned on a 17+. Contest

name: Eerie Spell
effects: The user may choose to Disable the target’s last Move used. This effect may only be activated once per Scene. Contest

name: Expanding Force
effects: After Expanding Force is resolved, you may create Psychic Terrain in a Blast 7, centered on the user, that lasts for 5 turns. Any creatures in those spaces are affected as if by the Field Move Psychic Terrain, rather than any other Field Move. This Effect may trigger only once per Scene. Contest

name: Freezing Glare
effects: Freezing Glare Freezes the target on a 19+. Once per Scene, Freezing Glare may instead be used as an Ice-Type Move. Contest

name: Magic Powder
effects: The target gains the Psychic Type in addition to its other Types for 5 turns. Contest

name: Meteor Beam
effects: The user’s Special Attack is raised by +1 Combat Stage. Resolution

name: Tar Shot
effects: The target gains a Coat that is automatically expended upon being hit with a Fire Type attack in order to make them one step more vulnerable to that attack. In addition, the target’s Speed is lowered by 1 CS. Contest

name: Behemoth Bash
effects: The DB of Behemoth Bash increases by +2 for each positive CS the target has, to a maximum of DB 20. Contest

name: Behemoth Blade
effects: The DB of Behemoth Blade increases by +2 for each positive CS the target has, to a maximum of DB 20. Contest

name: Double Iron Bash
effects: Double Iron Bash Flinches the targets on 15+. This move is affected by Iron Fist. Contest

name: Gear Up
effects: All targets that are Steel-Typed receive +1 Attack and Special Attack Combat Stages Contest

name: Steel Beam
effects: The user’s Hit Points are reduced by 50% of their full Hit Point value. This Hit Point loss cannot be prevented or reduced in any way. Contest

name: Steel Roller
range: Melee, Pass, Dash, Smite, Spirit Surge
effects: Legal targets hit by Steel Roller are Tripped on a roll of 15+. Any Hazards in spaces Passed through or adjacent to those spaces are removed (they are removed before they can affect the user). If the user moves through an area of Terrain, or a Field-range Terrain is active, those effects immediately end. Contest

name: Fishious Rend
effects: Against targets with a lower initiative that have not yet acted this round, Fishious Rend deals +10 damage. Contest

name: Flip Turn
effects: If Flip Turn successfully hits its target, the user deals damage and then immediately is returned to its Poké Ball in the same turn. A New Pokémon may immediately be sent out. Using Flip Turn lets a Trapped user be recalled. Contest

name: Life Dew
effects: The user and all allies in the burst regain Hit Points equal to 1/4 of their Maximum Hit Points. Contest

name: Snipe Shot
effects: Snipe Shot is a Critical Hit on 18+. This Move may not be Intercepted, nor may any Abilities, Moves, or Features be activated to change this Move’s target. Any of those existing effects (such as Follow Me) fail. Contest

name: Surging Strikes
effects: If Surging Strikes hits, it is a Critical Hit. After attacking with Surging Strikes, hit or miss, the user may Shift 2m, ignoring Attacks of Opportunity from their target. It may then make an additional attack with this Move on a different target. This effect may be repeated a second time, the third attack targeting a creature that has not yet been targeted by either prior attack. Before making each attack roll, the user can elect to give up triggering all remaining additional shifts and attacks. Surging Strikes gains +3 DB for each attack that is given up. Contest
